metadata.author: Griptape, Inc.
metadata.description: VOID is a two-pass video inpainting model that removes objects from videos along with all interactions they induce on the scene -- not just secondary effects like shadows and reflections, but physical interactions such as objects falling when a supporting person is removed. Pass 1 generates a plausible inpainted video, and Pass 2 (optional) refines temporal consistency using warped-noise from the Pass 1 output.

void_inpainting.title: VOID Inpainting
void_inpainting.description: Physics-aware video object removal with interaction simulation

metadata.description: Removes a masked object from a video using Netflix's VOID model. Takes a binary mask of the object and an optional mask of physically-affected regions, builds the VOID quadmask internally, runs Pass 1 inpainting, and optionally runs Pass 2 warped-noise refinement for improved temporal consistency.
